Class DatabaseManager

java.lang.Object
main.DatabaseManager
public class DatabaseManager extends Object
Central access point for all interactions with the SurrealDB database.

Implemented as a singleton, accessible via getInstance(). Wraps connection management, authentication, and every read/write operation used by the application, including user accounts, repairs, equipment, parts, notifications, and action logs.

Multi-statement writes that touch related tables (e.g. creating or updating a User and its associated RegistrableUser / Employee / Client records) are wrapped in explicit Transactions to keep them atomic.

    Class
    Description
    static final record 
    DatabaseManager.DbFunctionCall
    A single argument call to a server-side SurrealDB validation function, used to check whether a given field value satisfies a DB-defined rule (e.g.
    static final record 
    DatabaseManager.NotificationRequest
    Describes a notification to be created and delivered to a target, which may be a specific user's RecordId or a broad role such as "ADMIN".
    static final record 
    DatabaseManager.UserCredentials
    Login credentials supplied by a user attempting to sign in.

    Assigns an employee to a repair: sets the repair's state to ACCEPTED, relates the employee to the repair, and notifies the employee of the assignment.

    Closes the underlying database connection.

    Connects to the SurrealDB server using the connection details stored in the application's properties file, signs in with the configured root credentials, and selects the configured namespace and database.

    Retrieves the equipment submitted by a given user, optionally filtered by a case-insensitive substring match against brand or model, ordered alphabetically by brand.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static DatabaseManager getInstance()
      Returns the shared singleton instance of the database manager, creating it on first access.
      Returns:
      the singleton DatabaseManager instance

    • connect

      public void connect()
      Connects to the SurrealDB server using the connection details stored in the application's properties file, signs in with the configured root credentials, and selects the configured namespace and database.

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes the underlying database connection. Should be called once on application shutdown.

    • hasAdmin

      public boolean hasAdmin()
      Checks whether at least one active administrator account already exists in the database.
      Returns:
      true if an active admin user exists, false otherwise

    • isConfigured

      public boolean isConfigured()
      Checks whether the application's connection properties have already been configured (i.e. a previous successful setup has taken place).
      Returns:
      true if connection properties are present, false otherwise

    • saveUser

      public void saveUser(User user)
      Persists a new user to the database. Depending on the runtime type of user, also creates and links the associated registrable_user record and, for employees or clients, the corresponding employee/client record, via graph relations (is_a, of_type). The entire operation runs inside a single transaction and is rolled back on failure.
      Parameters:
      user - the user to create; may be an Admin, Employee, or Client
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if any part of the creation fails; the transaction is cancelled before the exception propagates

    • validateField

      public String validateField(DatabaseManager.DbFunctionCall call)
      Validates a single field's value against a server-side SurrealDB validation function.
      Parameters:
      call - the function name and value to validate
      Returns:
      null if the value is valid, or the database's error message describing why validation failed

    • userExists

      public boolean userExists(DatabaseManager.UserCredentials credentials)
      Checks whether a username/password combination matches an existing user account.
      Parameters:
      credentials - the username and password to check
      Returns:
      true if the credentials match a stored account, false otherwise

    • getUserStatus

      public String getUserStatus(String username)
      Retrieves the account status (e.g. PENDING, ACTIVE, REJECTED, INACTIVE) of the user with the given username.
      Parameters:
      username - the username to look up
      Returns:
      the user's current status

    • getType

      public String getType(String username)
      Retrieves the account type (e.g. ADMIN, EMPLOYEE, CLIENT) of the user with the given username.
      Parameters:
      username - the username to look up
      Returns:
      the user's account type

    • fetchUser

      public User fetchUser(String username)
      Fetches a fully-populated user (including registrable-user and type-specific fields, where applicable) by username.
      Parameters:
      username - the username to look up
      Returns:
      the matching User (as an Admin, Employee, or Client), or null if not found or on error

    • sendNotification

      public void sendNotification(DatabaseManager.NotificationRequest request)
      Creates and stores a new notification for the given target.
      Parameters:
      request - the notification's content and target recipient

    • getUnreadNotifications

      public long getUnreadNotifications(User user)
      Counts how many notifications addressed to the given user (either directly, by id, or by their role/type) have not yet been marked as viewed.
      Parameters:
      user - the user to count unread notifications for
      Returns:
      the number of unread notifications

    • getNotifications

      public List<Notification> getNotifications(User user, boolean listAll)
      Retrieves notifications addressed to the given user, ordered from most to least recent.
      Parameters:
      user - the user to retrieve notifications for
      listAll - if true, returns all notifications regardless of read status; if false, returns only unread ones
      Returns:
      the matching notifications, newest first

    • markAsRead

      public void markAsRead(User user, Notification notification)
      Marks a notification as viewed by the given user.
      Parameters:
      user - the user who viewed the notification
      notification - the notification that was viewed

    • getLogs

      public List<Log> getLogs(String search)
      Retrieves system action logs, optionally filtered by the acting user's name, ordered from most to least recent.
      Parameters:
      search - a case-insensitive substring to match against the acting user's name, or an empty string to return all logs
      Returns:
      the matching log entries, newest first

    • getLogsForRepair

      public List<Log> getLogsForRepair(com.surrealdb.RecordId repairId)
      Retrieves the full action history (e.g. submission, status changes) for a specific repair, ordered chronologically from oldest to newest.

      Matches log entries whose details text mentions the given repair's record id, since log entries reference repairs by embedding their id directly in the message rather than via a relation.

      Parameters:
      repairId - the id of the repair to retrieve the history for
      Returns:
      the matching log entries, oldest first

    • getUsers

      public List<User> getUsers(String search, User currUser)
      Retrieves all users other than currUser, optionally filtered by a case-insensitive substring match against name or username.
      Parameters:
      search - a substring to filter by, or an empty string to return all users
      currUser - the currently logged-in user, excluded from the results
      Returns:
      the matching users (as Admin, Employee, or Client)

    • setUserStatus

      public void setUserStatus(User user, String status)
      Updates the account status of the given user.
      Parameters:
      user - the user whose status should be changed
      status - the new status value (see UserStatus)

    • updateUser

      public void updateUser(User user)
      Persists changes to an existing user's data, including any type-specific fields (employee/client) and registrable-user fields, within a single transaction.

      Statements are issued bottom-up (type-specific table first, then registrable_user, then user last) to avoid traversing back through a table already accessed earlier in the same transaction, which SurrealDB does not resolve correctly.

      Each statement's result is explicitly consumed via take(0) so that per-statement database errors (e.g. failed field validation) are surfaced as exceptions rather than silently ignored.

      Parameters:
      user - the user with updated field values to persist
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if any statement fails; the transaction is cancelled before the exception propagates

    • savePart

      public void savePart(Part part)
      Persists a new part to the database.
      Parameters:
      part - the part to create

    • updatePart

      public void updatePart(Part part)
      Persists changes to an existing part by merging the given object's fields into the stored record.
      Parameters:
      part - the part with updated field values

    • getParts

      public List<Part> getParts(String search)
      Retrieves parts, optionally filtered by a case-insensitive substring match against designation or manufacturer.
      Parameters:
      search - a substring to filter by, or an empty string to return all parts
      Returns:
      the matching parts

    • fetchPart

      public Part fetchPart(com.surrealdb.RecordId id)
      Fetches a single part by id.
      Parameters:
      id - the part's record id
      Returns:
      the matching part
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if no part with the given id exists

    • saveEquipment

      public void saveEquipment(Equipment equipment, User user)
      Persists a new piece of equipment and relates it to the client who submitted it, within a single transaction. A unique SKU is generated (regenerated on collision) before saving.
      Parameters:
      equipment - the equipment to create
      user - the client submitting the equipment

    • getEquipment

      public List<Equipment> getEquipment(com.surrealdb.RecordId userId, String search)
      Retrieves the equipment submitted by a given user, optionally filtered by a case-insensitive substring match against brand or model, ordered alphabetically by brand.
      Parameters:
      userId - the id of the client whose equipment should be retrieved
      search - a substring to filter by, or an empty string to return all equipment
      Returns:
      the matching equipment, ordered by brand

    • saveRepair

      public void saveRepair(Equipment equipment, User user)
      Creates a new repair request for the given equipment on behalf of the given client, relating both, then notifies all admins of the new request.
      Parameters:
      equipment - the equipment the repair request is for
      user - the client submitting the request
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the transaction fails; it is cancelled before the exception propagates

    • getRepairs

      public List<Repair> getRepairs(String search, String filterState, ZonedDateTime startDate, ZonedDateTime endDate, User user)
      Retrieves repairs, scoped to the given user (all repairs for admins, only the user's own repairs otherwise), and optionally filtered by repair code or client name, status, and a start-date range. Each result's client name is resolved and attached afterwards.
      Parameters:
      search - a substring to match against repair code or client name
      filterState - a specific repair status to filter by, or an empty string to include all statuses
      startDate - if non-null, only include repairs starting on or after this date
      endDate - if non-null, only include repairs starting on or before this date
      user - the user requesting the list, used to scope results by role
      Returns:
      the matching repairs, each with its client name populated

    • fetchRepair

      public Repair fetchRepair(com.surrealdb.RecordId id)
      Fetches a single repair by id.
      Parameters:
      id - the repair's record id
      Returns:
      the matching repair

    • updateRepairState

      public void updateRepairState(Repair repair, String reason, String state, Double cost)
      Updates a repair's state, optionally recording a rejection reason or a final cost, and notifies the relevant party (the client, or all admins if an employee rejected the repair) of the change.
      Parameters:
      repair - the repair to update
      reason - the rejection reason, used only when state is REJECTED_BY_ADMIN or REJECTED_BY_EMPLOYEE; ignored otherwise
      state - the new repair state (see RepairStatus)
      cost - the final cost to record, or null if not applicable

    • getAvailableEmployees

      public List<Employee> getAvailableEmployees(com.surrealdb.RecordId repairId)
      Retrieves active employees who are not yet assigned to the given repair, available to be assigned.
      Parameters:
      repairId - the id of the repair to find available employees for
      Returns:
      the list of eligible employees

    • assignEmployee

      public void assignEmployee(Repair repair, Employee employee)
      Assigns an employee to a repair: sets the repair's state to ACCEPTED, relates the employee to the repair, and notifies the employee of the assignment. Runs within a single transaction.
      Parameters:
      repair - the repair to assign
      employee - the employee to assign to the repair
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the transaction fails; it is cancelled before the exception propagates
